edges.description: Reduced oxygen delivery or extraction is proposed to force skeletal muscle toward anaerobic metabolism and ionic stress during exertion.
edges.source: Impaired Peripheral Oxygen Extraction and Microcirculation

edges.description: Muscle ionic and energy disturbance may generate metabolites and stress signals that amplify immune, autonomic, and CNS symptoms after activity.
Immunometabolic amplification is proposed to explain delayed worsening of existing ME/CFS symptoms after exertion.

nodes.description: Activity-induced metabolites and oxidative mediators may trigger local and systemic immune activation, intensifying bioenergetic inflexibility, muscular ionic disturbance, autonomic dysfunction, and CNS symptom worsening after a delay.

evidence.reference_title: Towards an understanding of physical activity-induced post-exertional malaise: Insights into microvascular alterations and immunometabolic interactions in post-COVID condition and myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of molecules such as lactate, reactive oxygen species or prostaglandins might trigger local and systemic immune activation.

evidence.reference_title: Towards an understanding of physical activity-induced post-exertional malaise: Insights into microvascular alterations and immunometabolic interactions in post-COVID condition and myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Subsequent intensification of bioenergetic inflexibilities, muscular ionic disturbances and modulation of central nervous system functions can lead to an exacerbation of existing pathologies and symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Single-cell transcriptomics of the immune system in ME/CFS at baseline and following symptom provocation.
evidence.snippet: Comparing the transcriptome at baseline and postexercise challenge, we discover patterns indicative of improper platelet activation in patients, with minimal changes elsewhere in the immune system.

nodes.description: Skeletal muscle may undergo exertion-amplified mitochondrial, ionic, and tissue stress, including repeated damage and regeneration in some post-infectious ME/CFS patients, contributing to exertional intolerance and post-exertional malaise.

evidence.reference_title: Key Pathophysiological Role of Skeletal Muscle Disturbance in Post COVID and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS): Accumulated Evidence.
evidence.snippet: recent studies provide strong evidence for mitochondrial dysfunction in skeletal muscle tissue in ME/CFS.

evidence.reference_title: Key Pathophysiological Role of Skeletal Muscle Disturbance in Post COVID and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS): Accumulated Evidence.
evidence.snippet: This energy deficit is the most likely cause of exertional intolerance and post exertional malaise and is further aggravated by exertion.

nodes.description: Physical activity may unmask reduced systemic oxygen extraction and oxidative phosphorylation capacity, with microcirculatory and mitochondrial dysfunction maintained by latent immune activation and impairing peripheral bioenergetics.
nodes.id: Impaired Peripheral Oxygen Extraction and Microcirculation

evidence.reference_title: Towards an understanding of physical activity-induced post-exertional malaise: Insights into microvascular alterations and immunometabolic interactions in post-COVID condition and myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Upon physical activity, affected patients exhibit a reduced systemic oxygen extraction and oxidative phosphorylation capacity.

evidence.reference_title: Insights From Invasive Cardiopulmonary Exercise Testing of Patients With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These results identify two types of peripheral neurovascular dysregulation that are biologically plausible contributors to ME/CFS exertional intolerance-depressed Qc from impaired venous return, and impaired peripheral oxygen extraction.

nodes.description: Worsening of symptoms following physical or mental exertion, often delayed by 24-72 hours and lasting days to weeks.

evidence.snippet: Post-exertional malaise (PEM) is a cardinal symptom of the illnesses referred to as Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (ME), Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS), and chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS)
